title: Business Continuity — RTO Breach Signals from ITSI Service Degradation

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.5.29 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.5.29 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.14: Business Continuity — RTO Breach Signals from ITSI Service Degradation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.14' running on itsi_summary, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d is_service_max_severity_event=1
| lookup service_rto_minutes.csv serviceid OUTPUT rto_minutes
| eval breach=if(severity>=5 AND duration>rto_minutes,1,0)
| where breach=1
| stats count by serviceid
description: Uses service health episodes breaching RTO thresholds as objective continuity monitoring beyond paper plans.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsi_summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: Uses service health episodes breaching recovery time thresholds as objective continuity monitoring beyond paper plans — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Calibrate duration field to your ITSI KPIs; (2) align with BIA tiers; (3) BC manager monthly review. Indexes required: `index=itsi_summary`; Lookups: `service_rto_minutes`, `service_rto_minutes.csv`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `itsi_summary`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate duration field to your ITSI KPIs; (2) align with BIA tiers; (3) BC manager monthly review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d is_service_max_severity_event=1
| lookup service_rto_minutes.csv serviceid OUTPUT rto_minutes
| eval breach=if(severity>=5 AND duration>rto_minutes,1,0)
| where breach=1
| stats count by serviceid
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Continuity — RTO Breach Signals from ITSI Service Degradation (A.5.29)** — Uses service health episodes breaching RTO thresholds as objective continuity monitoring beyond paper plans.

Documented **Data sources**: `itsi_summary`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsi_summary.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsi_summary, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where breach=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by serviceid** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart, Table
knownFalsePositives: Access reviews lag during HR transitions, manager changes, or quarterly review cycles.
